mission.title: knative-eventing: Trigger without a broker does not correctly reflect ready status
mission.description: Trigger without a broker does not correctly reflect ready status. Community-reported issue.

steps.title: Identify knative-eventing troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your knative-eventing deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n knative-eventing -l app.kubernetes.io/name=knative-eventing
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=knative-eventing -n knative-eventing --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review knative-eventing configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant knative-eventing configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n knative-eventing -l app.kubernetes.io/name=knative-eventing
kubectl get configmap -n knative-eventing -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=knative-eventing
```
apply a trigger with a missing broker, the `READY` and `REASON` status fields have no info 

 It should be stated that the trigger is not `ready` and give a `reason`

 **To Reproduce**

apply a trigger, but have no broker

 **Knative release

steps.title: Apply the fix for Trigger without a broker does not correctly reflect ready…
steps.description:
- If trigger points to non-existent Broker, set status to failed with error message.
```yaml
{
  "level": "error",
  "ts": "2020-04-15T14:28:48.706Z",
  "logger": "broker-controller",
  "caller": "broker/reconciler.go:144",
  "msg": "resource \"default/default\" no longer exists",
  "commit": "02bc516",
  "knative.dev/traceid": "82fc04a2-31dc-476f-a3cf-abb1613925b1",
  "knative.dev/key": "default/default",
  "stacktrace": "knative.dev/eventing/pkg/client/injection/reconciler/eventing/v1alpha1/broker.(*reconcilerImpl).Reconcile\n\tknative.dev/eventing/pkg/client/injection/reconciler/eventing/v1alpha1/broker/reconciler.go:144\nknative.dev/eventing/vendor/knative.dev/pkg/controller.(*Imp
```

steps.title: Confirm Trigger without a broker does not correctly… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=knative-eventing -n knative-eventing --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n knative-eventing --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary: - If trigger points to non-existent Broker, set status to failed with error message.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with knative-eventing installed or the issue environment reproducible.
